title: Peristrophe pumila
taxonomy: Peristrophe pumila is a species within the plant family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Peristrophe and the family Acanthaceae under the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Peristrophe pumila is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Family: Acanthaceae, Genus: Peristrophe, Species: P. pumila. Peristrophe pumila is commonly known as the dwarf peristrophe or dwarf false bugloss. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant within its respective kingdom and family.
uses: Peristrophe pumila is reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. It is believed to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating infections. In some parts of the world, it is used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Additionally, it is thought to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Peristrophe pumila for medicinal purposes.

intro: Peristrophe pumila, also known as the dwarf peristrophe or small flower peristrophe, is a small, annual herb belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties and is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Peristrophe pumila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in the form of poultices and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
